{{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

{{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun}} *[http://www.city.fukui-sakai.lg.jp/bunka/kanko-bunka/bunka/bunkazai/rokurose-yama.html Sakai city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sakai-rokuroseyama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}
Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457440]] Wikidata name: Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | native_name = 六呂瀬山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG|image= File:Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|07|02.9|N|136|18|54.8|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. '''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457440]] Wikidata name: Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | native_name = 六呂瀬山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG|image= File:Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|07|02.9|N|136|18|54.8|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457440]] Wikidata name: Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | native_name = 六呂瀬山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG|image= File:Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|07|02.9|N|136|18|54.8|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun}} *[http://www.city.fukui-sakai.lg.jp/bunka/kanko-bunka/bunka/bunkazai/rokurose-yama.html Sakai city home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://info.pref.fukui.jp/bunka/bunkazai/sitei/siseki/sakai-rokuroseyama.html Cultural Heritage of Fukui Prefecture] {{in lang|ja}}

Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457440]] Wikidata name: Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | native_name = 六呂瀬山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG|image= File:Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|07|02.9|N|136|18|54.8|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location =[[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the [[:en:Maruoka, Fukui|Maruoka]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Sakai, Fukui|Sakai, Fukui]] in the [[:en:Hokuriku region|Hokuriku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the [[:en:Kuzuryū River|Kuzuryū River]]. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] boat-shaped [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped tumulus]] located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from [[:en:Shiizakai Station|Shiizakai Station]] on the [[:en:Echizen Railway|Echizen Railway]] [[:en:Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Sakai, Fukui|en|Sakai, Fukui|WD=Q990342|lt=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Maruoka, Fukui|en|Maruoka, Fukui|WD=Q177273|lt=Maruoka}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakai, Fukui|en|Sakai, Fukui|WD=Q990342|lt=Sakai, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the {{ill|Kuzuryū River|en|Kuzuryū River|WD=Q6449325|lt=Kuzuryū River}}. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} boat-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Shiizakai Station|en|Shiizakai Station|WD=Q7496501|lt=Shiizakai Station}} on the {{ill|Echizen Railway|en|Echizen Railway|WD=Q1007890|lt=Echizen Railway}} {{ill|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|en|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|WD=Q6378238|lt=Katsuyama Eiheiji Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuroseyama Kofun]][[en:Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster]][[ja:六呂瀬山古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18457440]] Wikidata name: Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description| part_of of kofun Kofuns in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | native_name = 六呂瀬山古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image =Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG|image= File:Rokuroseyama kofun.JPG| image_size = | alt = | caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Fukui Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | relief = 1 | map_caption = Rokuroseiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|36|07|02.9|N|136|18|54.8|E|display=inline}}; {{Coord|36.117472|N|136.315222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location ={{ill|Sakai, Fukui|en|Sakai, Fukui|WD=Q990342|lt=Sakai, Fukui}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}; {{ill|Sakai|WD=Q990342}}| region = {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 4th or early 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster'''|六呂瀬山古墳群|Rokuroseiyama Kofun-gun}} is a group ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]]s located in the {{ill|Maruoka, Fukui|en|Maruoka, Fukui|WD=Q177273|lt=Maruoka}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Sakai, Fukui|en|Sakai, Fukui|WD=Q990342|lt=Sakai, Fukui}} in the {{ill|Hokuriku region|en|Hokuriku region|WD=Q381025|lt=Hokuriku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the {{ill|Kuzuryū River|en|Kuzuryū River|WD=Q6449325|lt=Kuzuryū River}}. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} boat-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Shiizakai Station|en|Shiizakai Station|WD=Q7496501|lt=Shiizakai Station}} on the {{ill|Echizen Railway|en|Echizen Railway|WD=Q1007890|lt=Echizen Railway}} {{ill|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|en|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|WD=Q6378238|lt=Katsuyama Eiheiji Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)|WD=Q10222682|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukui)}}

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1990.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138264 |title=六呂瀬山古墳群|trans-title= Rokuroseiyama kofun gun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is located on a hillside at an elevation of 50 to 200 meters above sea level, and consists of at least 130 circular, rectangular and keyhole-shape tumuli, collectively known as the {{nihongo|'''Maruoka Kofun Cluster'''|丸岡古墳群|Maruoka Kofun-gun}}, of which the Rokuroseyama Kofun Cluster is the portion towards the southeast of the site, at the highest elevation, overlooking the {{ill|Kuzuryū River|en|Kuzuryū River|WD=Q6449325|lt=Kuzuryū River}}. The site consists of two very large {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above (designated No.1 and No.3), and two {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular-type|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''hōfun''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}), designated No. 2 and No. 4). The tumuli are estimated to date from the late 4th to the early 5th century AD. The tombs do not appear in any historical records and the name of Rank of the person buried within is unknown.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==The Kofun== '''Kofun No.1''' is located at an elevation of 200 meters at the ridgeline of the hill, with a north-south orientation. With a total length of 147 meters, it is the largest found in the Hokuriku region. The posterior circular portion of the tomb in on the northern end, and has a diameter of 78 meters and a height of 13 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 52 meters, width of 58 meters and height of 11 meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''[[fukiishi]]'' and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' in cylindrical, morning-glory, or house shapes, were found in the area. The circular portion contains a semi-circular [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with dimensions of 27 x 15 meters with a height of 4.2 meters, which had been broken into and robbed at some indeterminate time. Fragments of a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} boat-shaped {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} were found scattered on the floor.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.2''' is a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 16 meters east-west by 11 meters north-south, and a height of 2.2 meters and is located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.1, but contained no ''fukiishi'' and no ''haniwa''.
'''Kofun No.3''' is also a large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus}} located at the ridgeline of the hill, but with an east-west orientation. It has a length of 85 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 48 meters and a height of 11 meters. The anterior rectangular portion has a length of 37 meters, width of 48 meters and height of nine meters in two tiers. Large numbers of ''fukuiishi'', as well as a large variety of ''haniwa'' (cylindrical, house-shaped, trapezoidal, etc.) were found. The burial chamber is orientated to the north, with dimensions of 11 by 11 meters and a height of 1.1 meters. The tumulus appears to have been built later than Kofun No.1.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
'''Kofun No.4''' is also a {{ill|Square Kofun|lt=rectangular tumulus|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} with dimensions of 13 meters east-west by 16 meters north-south, and a height of 2.7 meters, located to the east of Kofun No.1. It appears to have been an auxiliary structure to Kofun No.3.
The site is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Shiizakai Station|en|Shiizakai Station|WD=Q7496501|lt=Shiizakai Station}} on the {{ill|Echizen Railway|en|Echizen Railway|WD=Q1007890|lt=Echizen Railway}} {{ill|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|en|Katsuyama Eiheiji Line|WD=Q6378238|lt=Katsuyama Eiheiji Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
